Figure 6: Training results for the four level SP-LPMA GUNet over 200 epochs.

Figure 1: An illustration of the overlapping graph partition: the vertex set V is first partitioned into disjoint subsets VI,1 and VI,2 (shaded regions). The local vertex sets V δ 1 and V δ 2 (outlined regions) are then formed by including the halo vertices V δ Γ,i within graph distance δ.

Figure 12: Performance on a 3D heterogeneous log-normal permeability field. Left: permeability realization κ(x) generated from a Gaussian random field Z = log κ with exponential covariance (Section 4.2.2) on a 256×256×256 mesh. Middle/Right: time-to-accuracy comparison between ADD and NN for C1 (vertical flow) and C2 (horizontal flow). Curves report relative error versus cumulative time (setup + solve); dotted lines mark the end of setup and the dashed line denotes the target tolerance 10−8.

Figure 11: Performance on a 2D high-contrast discrete fracture network (DFN). Left: Permeability realization κ(x) featuring randomly distributed high-conductivity fractures (channels with κ = 105) embedded in a homogeneous background matrix on a 2048× 2048 mesh. Middle/Right: Time-to-accuracy comparison between ADD and NN for C1 (vertical flow) and C2 (horizontal flow). Curves report relative error versus cumulative time (setup + solve); dotted lines mark the end of setup and the dashed line denotes the target tolerance 10−8.
